verdict.summary: Phase 0 research, inventory, corpus, contract, and risk-prototype work can start. Phase 1 and broad parallel implementation are blocked by unresolved compatibility, dependency, source-model, resource, durability, FFmpeg, update, and executable-acceptance contracts.

fixed_operator_intent.required_external_dependency: FFmpeg and ffprobe as supervised external processes
fixed_operator_intent.optimization_priority: Speed first among correctness-, parity-, bounded-resource-, and recoverability-valid designs
fixed_operator_intent.compatibility_foundation: yt-dlp behavior and source research, without a production yt-dlp runtime dependency

consensus_blockers.title: Rust-plus-FFmpeg dependency boundary conflicts with unproven JS, transport, plugin, and storage choices
consensus_blockers.required_resolution: Run Rust-only challenge, transport, process-plugin, and storage spikes; request a specific operator exception only if measured evidence proves it necessary.

consensus_blockers.title: The yt-dlp compatibility target is not enumerable or executable
consensus_blockers.required_resolution: Generate a pinned compatibility profile and mandatory oracle corpus covering invocation, source graph, sanitized network transcript, artifacts, failures, and archive semantics.

consensus_blockers.title: Current performance gates can reward inequivalent work or process topology
consensus_blockers.required_resolution: Require equivalent work first, then compare versioned workloads using distributions and confidence bounds.

consensus_blockers.title: The source model is not serializable and the main sequence places JS too late
consensus_blockers.required_resolution: Use a canonical serializable source graph with runtime cursors separated from persisted data, and expose JS/transport services during extraction.

consensus_blockers.title: Multi-resource admission and byte backpressure are incomplete
consensus_blockers.required_resolution: Specify an atomic resource-vector broker, byte-credit queues, structured ownership, cancellation, and fixed policies before adaptive behavior.

consensus_blockers.title: Commit, resume, archive, filesystem, and path operations lack a crash-safe state machine
consensus_blockers.required_resolution: Define durable staged commit records, no-follow root-relative path operations, filesystem capability modes, and startup reconciliation for every state prefix.

consensus_blockers.title: FFmpeg supervision is weaker than the required security and recovery boundary
consensus_blockers.required_resolution: Add trusted executable selection, version/features, environment/protocol restrictions, bounded pipe handling, process containment, cancellation, reaping, and ffprobe validation.

consensus_blockers.title: Extractor and challenge updateability is deferred too late
consensus_blockers.required_resolution: Move signed, independently versioned compatibility/update contracts with rollback and quarantine into Phase 0/1.

consensus_blockers.title: Acceptance gates are qualitative rather than executable
consensus_blockers.required_resolution: Give every gate a command, corpus version, expected artifact, threshold, timeout, failure oracle, and machine-readable report.

adjudicated_disagreements.resolution: Rust executable process IPC is the authorized v1 direction. Wasm requires a new runtime and therefore explicit operator approval.
The current SQLite default is unapproved. Benchmark and fault-test a maintained pure-Rust candidate first; request a narrow SQLite exception only with evidence.
Only a bounded Rust worker with a pure-Rust engine candidate is currently authorized. Corpus failure must be returned to the operator as measured evidence, not bypassed with an external runtime.
Retain in scope; enable as opt-in only after record-then-mux crash and recovery behavior passes.

peer_question_resolutions.resolution: Native Ferric semantic contracts plus a generated yt-dlp 2026.07.04 profile and oracle corpus.
Typed core plus bounded namespaced extensions; behavior and identity fields remain typed; persisted data is fully serializable.
V1 plugins are Rust executables over bounded versioned process IPC; Wasm needs explicit future approval.
Extend transport with streaming, cancellation, pooling identity, DNS/SSRF, redirects, cookies, fingerprints, replay, and sanitized transcripts.
Impersonation blocks only claims requiring it, but full replacement cannot pass while a mandatory case needs it.
Rust worker plus pure-Rust JS engine corpus spike; no external JS runtime is authorized.
Per-job no-capability contexts, deterministic bounded IPC, resource/recursion/time limits, worker recycling, OS containment, and quarantine.
Use byte credits and separate received, validated/written, and durable-contiguous checkpoints.
Specify and test exact local-filesystem guarantees; reject or explicitly degrade unsupported/network modes until proven.
Budget FFmpeg process slots plus aggregate CPU, memory, disk, and pipe use; transcodes receive stricter admission.
Logical source order controls identity/naming; completion-order progress must also carry logical sequence and stable identity.
Equivalent-work correctness first; versioned statistical performance manifest second.
Generate the full CLI/config inventory; replace unsafe arbitrary command behavior with typed Rust equivalents or approved divergences.
Pass 100% of the mandatory deterministic corpus and every active pinned extractor URL class, or record an explicitly approved divergence.
Use trusted absolute FFmpeg/ffprobe paths or an operator-chosen verified bundle; no JS runtime is approved; storage remains undecided.
Preserve logical boundaries but start with coarse crates; split only on security, lifecycle, public API, or measured ownership/build evidence.
Define job/cancel, source/redirect, admission, fragment/durability, live, sink, FFmpeg, JS, plugin, and commit/archive state machines.
Fuzz protocol manifests, templates/selectors/paths, journals, IPC/JS/plugin frames, cookies, and FFmpeg progress with allocation/time bounds.
No Python production bridge; port in-scope behavior to Rust and the Rust plugin protocol.
Signed stable/hotfix channels, separately versioned data/protocols, atomic activation, rollback, quarantine, last-known-good, and receipts; Rust logic ships in binaries.
Internal enums may be views; the canonical persisted and IPC model is a serializable graph.
Emit only listing-known fields early with explicit tri-state semantics; resolve asset fields later and expose avoided requests.
SQLite is unapproved; test a pure-Rust store; separate item/representation/track/asset/derived identities and archive only reconcilable committed outputs.
Use bounded trusted Rust fanout with lossless recording priority; isolate untrusted or blocking sinks.
All listed HLS/DASH/MSS/live/range/encryption/discontinuity/recording/mux behavior remains mandatory; real-time mux follows recovery proof.

blocked_by: unproven crash-safe storage and commit contracts

implementation_readiness_gates: Generated versioned yt-dlp compatibility profile and mandatory corpus
Accepted public behavior, dependency, security, identity, durability, and performance decisions
Approved Rust transport fingerprint, redirect, cookie, SSRF, replay, streaming, pooling, and cancellation result
Archive store crash, concurrency, migration, lease, and reconciliation result
Versioned serializable source graph, identity, plugin IPC, event, error, cancellation, and process protocols
Atomic resource-vector and byte-credit invariants with executable saturation and cancellation tests
Commit/archive/resume fault injection across every durable state prefix
FFmpeg supervision failure and descendant-process tests on Windows and Unix
Equivalent-work benchmark manifests with correctness gates and confidence intervals
Executable phase gates with machine-readable reports
No-context model manual, structured diagnostics, stable identifiers, and parallel-agent-safe receipts

recommended_next_closure_unit.name: Phase 0 contract and risk package
outputs: Generated yt-dlp 2026.07.04 compatibility profile and representative mandatory corpus
Pure-Rust archive-store versus SQLite evidence matrix without pre-approving SQLite
Direct-HTTP plus HLS plus FFmpeg-stream-copy vertical slice with cancel, resume, crash reconciliation, and equivalent-work benchmark
